---
title: "03 — Baseline Models"
author: "PSTAT 134 — AI Writing Detector"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-location: left
    embed-resources: true
    theme: simplex
    code-fold: show
execute:
  warning: false
  message: false
---

```{python}
#| label: setup
import sys
from pathlib import Path

PROJECT_ROOT = Path.cwd().parent if Path.cwd().name == "notebooks" else Path.cwd()
sys.path.append(str(PROJECT_ROOT))

import pandas as pd
from sklearn.linear_model import LogisticRegression
from sklearn.naive_bayes import MultinomialNB
from sklearn.pipeline import Pipeline

from src.features import build_tfidf_vectorizer
from src.evaluation import cv_summary
```

## Load processed training data

```{python}
train_df = pd.read_csv(PROJECT_ROOT / "data" / "processed" / "train.csv")
X_train, y_train = train_df["text"], train_df["generated"]
X_train.shape, y_train.shape
```

## Baseline 1: TF-IDF + Logistic Regression

The vectorizer lives inside the pipeline so it is fit only on the training
fold during cross-validation. This avoids leaking test-fold vocabulary into
training.

```{python}
logreg = Pipeline([
    ("tfidf", build_tfidf_vectorizer()),
    ("clf", LogisticRegression(max_iter=1000, n_jobs=-1)),
])

logreg_cv = cv_summary(logreg, X_train, y_train)
logreg_cv
```

## Baseline 2: TF-IDF + Multinomial Naive Bayes

```{python}
nb = Pipeline([
    ("tfidf", build_tfidf_vectorizer()),
    ("clf", MultinomialNB()),
])

nb_cv = cv_summary(nb, X_train, y_train)
nb_cv
```

## Side-by-side comparison

```{python}
comparison = pd.concat(
    {"logreg": logreg_cv, "naive_bayes": nb_cv}, axis=1
)
comparison
```

## Notes

- These numbers are 5-fold CV means/std on the **training** set only.
- The test set is still untouched — we report final numbers in notebook 04.
- If one baseline is dramatically ahead, note it here and bring it forward as
  the model to tune.
